violations: Style guard risk: multiple sentences likely exceed 28 words (e.g., in ## Context definitions and in ## Solution diagram introduction) and should be split.

System breakdown rubric risk: the four layers are listed and diagrammed, but the chapter does not explicitly state how a reader should implement boundaries (inputs/outputs) between layers beyond “write rules.”

>=2_examples rubric risk: there is one extended walkthrough plus short per-layer examples; the chapter would be clearer if a second distinct example scenario (non-bugfix) were included, but heading allowlist may constrain this in the next pass.
Mermaid diagram label issue: node A text appears to have an unmatched bracket/quote ("scope: one run" line lacks a closing bracket/quote symmetry compared to other nodes).

Style guard risk: the sentence “The takeaway: make “what the agent knows” a controlled blend of (1) volatile scratch, (2) explicit task state, (3) verified durable facts, and (4) derived retrieval—each with different write permissions.” likely exceeds 28 words and should be split.
Measurability gap: the second scenario (non-bugfix) does not state a checkable verification outcome (e.g., a named drill/test run ID or pass/fail criterion) beyond “successful drill.”
